channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump Confirms Iran Sent Oil-Linked Gift Amid Ongoing Diplomatic Talks
item.description: For Tuesday, President Donald Trump yarn say Iran don send United States one kain special gift wey concern oil and gas, and e help push diplomatic talks wey fit end wahala. Trump no wan yan wetin the ...

item.title: Young Africans, Simba, and Azam Battle for Lead in Tanzania's Premier League
item.description: The Tanzania Mainland Premier League is currently experiencing an intense title race as defending champions Young Africans (Yanga) sit at the top of the table with 38 points from 16 matches. They are ...

item.title: Rising Costs and Tax Pressure Affect Gig Workers' Take-Home Pay
item.description: Gig workers in South Africa are increasingly taking on multiple jobs to keep up with the rising costs of living, including food, transport, and rent. While their gross income may increase, many find t...

item.title: CERN Successfully Tests Antimatter Transport System
item.description: CERN scientists have achieved a groundbreaking milestone by successfully transporting antimatter, specifically antiprotons, over a 10-kilometre stretch on their campus. This world-first test drive of ...

item.title: House Committee Turns Over Classified Records to Prosecutors in Brennan Investigation Ahead of Charging Decision
item.description: Federal prosecutors are set to receive classified documents from the House Intelligence Committee as part of the ongoing Justice Department investigation into former CIA Director John Brennan. The com...

item.title: Tezgam train derails near Bahawalpur, injuring 26 passengers
item.description: A serious train accident occurred on Tuesday night when seven coaches of the Karachi-bound Tezgam Express derailed near Adam Wahan Railway Station on the Bahawalpur–Lodhran section. The train, travell...

item.title: Chhattisgarh’s Bilaspur Poultry Farm Sees 4,400 Chickens Die from Bird Flu, Surveillance Strengthened
item.description: In Bilaspur district of Chhattisgarh, at least 4,400 chickens have succumbed to an outbreak of avian influenza at a government poultry farm located in the Koni area. The Veterinary Department, led by ...

item.title: Customs seizes unregistered vape products worth over P1 billion in Navotas
item.description: The Bureau of Customs (BOC) has seized a large haul of illegal e-cigarettes and vape products worth more than ₱1 billion in Navotas City. The operation, conducted last March 21, led to the confiscatio...

item.title: Rising fuel prices drive South Africans towards hybrid vehicles
item.description: Amid rising petrol prices in South Africa, many motorists are rethinking their next vehicle purchase. According to Yazi's March 2026 SA Fuel & Energy Sentiment Study, a growing number of consumers are...

item.title: Mexican man arrested for illegally transporting family across Niagara River
item.description: On March 15, U.S. Border Patrol agents intercepted a Mexican national, Andres Carrillo-Hernandez, attempting to smuggle two adults and an infant from Canada into the United States using a small rubber...

item.title: Proteas youngsters gain vital experience in NZ T20 series, says Conrad
item.description: Shukri Conrad, the Proteas' head coach, reflected on the importance of South Africa's T20 series against New Zealand, despite some concerns about its relevance post-World Cup. The series, which follow...

item.title: Public advised to avoid close contact with lambs following cryptosporidium outbreak
item.description: Health authorities in Wales are urging families to avoid handling lambs during farm visits after a parasitic infection outbreak linked to petting farms last year left over 80 people ill. The infection...

item.title: Chalmers addresses rising inflation, warns of longer impacts from Middle East conflict
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has acknowledged rising inflation figures, attributing part of the pressure to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. While inflation had eased slightly in February, Chalmers ...

item.title: The Legal Implications of Section 129 in Raphael Tuju's Case
item.description: The arrest of former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju has brought Section 129 of Kenya's Penal Code into the limelight. This section of the law, which punishes the giving of false information to public ...

item.title: Two Air Canada Pilots Tragically Killed in LaGuardia Runway Collision
item.description: Antoine Forest and Mackenzie Gunther, two Air Canada Express pilots, have been identified as the victims of the tragic runway collision at New York City's LaGuardia Airport on Sunday. The accident occ...

item.title: Volkswagen Considers Converting Osnabrück Plant to Produce Iron Dome Components
item.description: Volkswagen is reportedly in discussions with Israel's Rafael Advanced Defence Systems to transform its Osnabrück factory from a car manufacturing plant into a facility that produces components for the...

item.title: INEC Calls for Staff Discipline and Integrity Ahead of Ekiti and Osun Governorship Elections
item.description: The Chairman of INEC, Prof. Joash Amupitan, don advise all INEC staff across Nigeria make dem dey disciplined, honest, and professional as Ramadan don pass, in preparation for the 2026 governorship el...

item.title: Rising Oil Prices and Insolvencies Threaten Australia’s Economic Stability
item.description: Australia’s economy is under mounting pressure as business insolvencies surge to record highs, driven by rising oil prices linked to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The situation has placed k...

item.title: Sara Duterte criticizes House for politicizing impeachment process amid national concerns
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte did not attend the formal start of the impeachment hearing against her in the House of Representatives, choosing instead to issue a public statement criticizing what she de...

item.title: PNP in Full Alert After Marcos Declares Energy Crisis
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has announced it is on heightened readiness following President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.'s declaration of a national energy emergency on March 25, 2026. The declaratio...
